---
title: SmallDocs cells - v1 examples
tags: [sheets-v1, demo]
---

# SmallDocs `cells` - v1 (sheets-v1)

Read-only inline sheets from a fenced `cells` block. Each block is parsed
into a grid model of `{raw, value, type}` cells and drawn as a CSS grid.
Below is every variation that exists today, so you can see exactly where
we are. Toggle dark mode (the moon, top bar) to see the theming; export
to HTML/PDF turns each grid into a real table.

---

## 1. Basic sheet

The teardown demo as plain inline data. Note there is no special "header
row" concept yet - every row is just a row, numbered from 1, columns
lettered from A. That keeps the model pure; a header style is a later knob.

```cells
Region,Q1,Q2,Q3
North,100,150,130
South,90,95,-7
East,120,80,60
```

---

## 2. Type detection & alignment

Numbers detect automatically and align right; text aligns left; blank
fields are empty cells. Negatives and decimals are numbers too. All
content is vertically centred in its row.

```cells
Item,Qty,Unit price,In stock
Widget,1200,3.50,yes
Gadget,-5,12.99,no
Spare,,0.00,
Bolt,42,,yes
```

---

## 3. Quoted fields (full CSV)

Commas inside quotes stay in one cell, `""` is an escaped quote, and a
quoted value like `"1,200"` stays text (no locale guessing in v1).

```cells
name,quote,amount
"Smith, Jordan","she said ""hi""","1,200"
"'Neil, A.","plain note",980
```

---

## 4. Ragged rows pad to a rectangle

Short rows are padded with empty cells so the grid is always rectangular.

```cells
a,b,c,d
1,2
x,y,z
only-one
```

---

## 5. Wrapping - long text and line breaks

A long value wraps once a column reaches its cap (rather than running the
column ever wider), growing the row. A literal `<br>` becomes a line
break, and a quoted cell with a real newline keeps it. Everything stays
plain text - markup never renders.

```cells
field,value
Long,sdfsdfsdfdfdsfsdfsdfsdfsfsdfsfsadfdsafsfsddsf and yet more text here
Breaks,one<br>two<br>three
Multi,"alpha
beta gamma"
Safe,"<b>not bold</b> stays text"
```

---

## 6. Wide sheet - horizontal scroll

24 columns. The grid hugs its content and scrolls sideways inside its own
box (the note / status strip below stays pinned). Only the cells move.

```cells
Metric,Jan 24,Feb 24,Mar 24,Apr 24,May 24,Jun 24,Jul 24,Aug 24,Sep 24,Oct 24,Nov 24,Dec 24,Jan 25,Feb 25,Mar 25,Apr 25,May 25,Jun 25,Jul 25,Aug 25,Sep 25,Oct 25,Nov 25,Dec 25
Revenue,100,107,114,121,128,135,142,149,156,163,170,177,184,191,198,205,212,219,226,233,240,247,254,261
COGS,40,43,46,49,52,55,58,61,64,67,70,73,76,79,82,85,88,91,94,97,100,103,106,109
```

---

## 7. Big sheet - bounded preview + truncation note

A 230-column block. The inline preview caps the painted grid (200 cols
here) and reports what it clipped, so a trimmed sheet never reads as
complete. Full windowing (pick a range, expand to fullscreen) is the next
rung. Scroll it sideways - the note stays put.

```cells
row,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7,c8,c9,c10,c11,c12,c13,c14,c15,c16,c17,c18,c19,c20,c21,c22,c23,c24,c25,c26,c27,c28,c29,c30,c31,c32,c33,c34,c35,c36,c37,c38,c39,c40,c41,c42,c43,c44,c45,c46,c47,c48,c49,c50,c51,c52,c53,c54,c55,c56,c57,c58,c59,c60,c61,c62,c63,c64,c65,c66,c67,c68,c69,c70,c71,c72,c73,c74,c75,c76,c77,c78,c79,c80,c81,c82,c83,c84,c85,c86,c87,c88,c89,c90,c91,c92,c93,c94,c95,c96,c97,c98,c99,c100,c101,c102,c103,c104,c105,c106,c107,c108,c109,c110,c111,c112,c113,c114,c115,c116,c117,c118,c119,c120,c121,c122,c123,c124,c125,c126,c127,c128,c129,c130,c131,c132,c133,c134,c135,c136,c137,c138,c139,c140,c141,c142,c143,c144,c145,c146,c147,c148,c149,c150,c151,c152,c153,c154,c155,c156,c157,c158,c159,c160,c161,c162,c163,c164,c165,c166,c167,c168,c169,c170,c171,c172,c173,c174,c175,c176,c177,c178,c179,c180,c181,c182,c183,c184,c185,c186,c187,c188,c189,c190,c191,c192,c193,c194,c195,c196,c197,c198,c199,c200,c201,c202,c203,c204,c205,c206,c207,c208,c209,c210,c211,c212,c213,c214,c215,c216,c217,c218,c219,c220,c221,c222,c223,c224,c225,c226,c227,c228,c229,c230
```

## 8. Empty block - inline error

An empty `cells` block surfaces an inline error instead of a blank grid.

```cells
   
```

---

That is the whole v1 surface. Next: a fullscreen overlay reusing this same
renderer, then CSV-file sourcing, then editing and formulas - each clips
onto this spine without changing it.
